home.testimonial3_text: Serious and professional work. The website exactly reflects the image we wanted to give to our brand. Bilal understood our vision and translated it visually.

about.title: About — Bilal Essatte
about.metaDesc: Bilal Essatte — Graphic & Web Designer based in Tangier, Morocco. Self-taught since 2016, agency experience at WeProd Maroc.
about.missionQuote: I don't just design. I solve problems — wherever you put me, I adapt.
about.exp1_desc: Web interface creation and graphic design for a culinary platform.
about.exp2_desc: Notable projects: Flamant Rose Apartments, Hotel Farah Tanger, Palais Blanc, Bloom Beauty Tanger. Creation of digital and print communication materials for the hospitality and lifestyle sector.
about.exp3_desc: Supporting over 50 international and local clients in creating complete visual ecosystems (branding, web, social media).

weprod.title: WeProd Maroc - Graphic Designer | Bilal Essatte
weprod.metaDesc: My first experience inside a creative agency: branding, social media, print and event communication for WeProd Maroc.
weprod.heroDesc: My first experience inside a creative agency, where I worked on branding, social media, print design and event communication for hotels, restaurants and local brands.
weprod.aboutDesc1: WeProd Maroc is one of the leading communication and event agencies in Tangier. The agency works with hotels, restaurants, beauty brands and companies, creating everything from social media campaigns to large events, branding and print materials.
weprod.aboutDesc2: This was my first experience working inside a professional creative agency. It completely changed the way I see design, teamwork and communication.
weprod.joinedDesc1: Before joining WeProd, I was working as a freelance graphic designer. Freelancing alone wasn't giving me enough stability, and I wanted real experience inside a creative agency.
weprod.joinedDesc2: A friend who worked at WeProd told me they were looking for a graphic designer. I applied, heard nothing for a few days, and almost forgot about it. Then one morning I woke up to several missed calls. When they called back, the first thing I heard was: "Bilal, I think you don't really want this job!" We both laughed, and a few days later I had my very first job interview.
weprod.joinedDesc3: I passed the interviews with the Community Manager, HR and the founder, and started working that same day.
weprod.didItem1: Designed social media posts and stories
weprod.didItem6: Created branding assets for multiple clients
weprod.didItem8: Worked with community managers, videographers and a 3D designer
weprod.vehicleDesc: I also designed the full branding for WeProd's own company vehicle — used for events and equipment transport across Tangier. The design included the WeProd logo, contact details, a small map showing the agency's headquarters and operation sites, and a visual built around the agency's core services: sound, stage, lighting and event structures.
weprod.catalogueDesc: I designed a catalogue covering all the equipment and services used by WeProd's event team: tents and stands (chapiteaux), electrical setup, lighting, sound, audiovisual equipment, decor and furniture, scenography, and printing.
weprod.campaignsDesc: Beyond client work, I also created social media content for WeProd's own brand — including an Instagram story supporting the Moroccan national football team, and promotional visuals for the Auto Expo event. The third image is a mockup showing how one of these campaigns looked live on Instagram.
weprod.featuredDesc1: The project I'm most proud of at WeProd was a large, traditional women-only event. I had only just joined the agency, but during a team meeting the founder asked everyone for marketing ideas — so I proposed one.
weprod.featuredDesc2: I called it the "Blind Ticket": people could buy their ticket before the guest artist was announced. It created curiosity and got people talking about the event early.
weprod.featuredDesc3: Once the idea was approved, I designed the full campaign around it: the sponsorship dossier, roll-up banners, event tickets, artist announcements and all the Instagram content.
weprod.learnedQuote: "Keep It Stupid Simple" — a colleague taught me this early on, and it changed how I approach every design since.
weprod.learnedItem1: How a real creative agency works day to day
weprod.learnedItem2: How to prepare files for large-format printing
weprod.learnedItem3: How to build a full campaign, not just single posts
weprod.learnedItem4: How to stay creative under tight, repeating deadlines
weprod.learnedItem5: How to pitch an idea to a founder and see it become real
weprod.reflectionDesc: I joined WeProd mostly thinking of myself as a graphic designer. I left with a much broader view — of branding, print production, event marketing and working as part of a team. This experience confirmed that I enjoy adapting quickly to new challenges and industries.

hotelfarah.title: Hotel Farah Tanger - Graphic Designer | Bilal Essatte
hotelfarah.metaDesc: Luxury hospitality social media and print design for Hotel Farah Tanger, created during my time at WeProd Maroc.
hotelfarah.heroDesc: Social media and print design for one of Tangier's best-known luxury hotels — created while working at WeProd Maroc.
hotelfarah.aboutDesc1: Hotel Farah Tanger is a well-known luxury hotel in Tangier. As part of my work at WeProd Maroc, I created ongoing social media and promotional content to keep the hotel's brand image consistent and premium across Instagram and Facebook.
hotelfarah.aboutDesc2: The content covered everything from event promotions like Karaoke Night at Le Jazz Bar, to seasonal offers at El Chiringuito, national holiday posts, and Pool Day Pass promotions.
hotelfarah.designedItem1: Instagram posts for events and promotions
hotelfarah.challengesDesc1: The biggest challenge wasn't the design itself — it was staying creative with limited source material. The hotel's rooms, pool and restaurant stayed the same, so every new post needed a fresh angle without repeating the last one.
hotelfarah.challengesDesc2: Communication with the hotel's team wasn't always clear, which meant more back-and-forth than usual to get each piece approved.
hotelfarah.lookbackDesc: This project taught me how to keep a luxury brand feeling consistent across dozens of posts, how to adapt one idea into multiple formats, and how to keep producing quality content on a tight, repeating schedule — even when feedback wasn't always clear.
hotelfarah.galleryDesc: A selection of social media and print designs created for Hotel Farah Tanger.

smartspares.title: Smart Spares Hub - Brand & Web Designer | Bilal Essatte
smartspares.metaDesc: A premium B2B platform for industrial spare parts — branding, web design and development from scratch.
smartspares.heroDesc: A B2B platform built from scratch for industrial spare parts sourcing — branding, web design, development and SEO, all designed and built by me.
smartspares.aboutDesc1: Smart Spares Hub is a B2B platform for industrial spare parts and maintenance solutions. My cousin, who runs the business, asked me to build the company's first website — his goal was a premium experience, inspired by the Apple website, that would make procurement simple for industrial clients.
smartspares.aboutDesc2: I designed and built the entire project alone: brand identity, UI design, WordPress development, and a printed roll-up for the business.
smartspares.goalDesc: Build the company's first website, with a premium image inspired by Apple's design language. The site needed to present industrial products clearly, build trust with B2B clients, and support the business as it grows.
smartspares.builtItem3: Product search and category navigation system
smartspares.builtItem6: Responsive design for mobile and desktop
smartspares.builtItem7: SEO setup: titles, meta descriptions, alt text, sitemap, internal linking
smartspares.identityDesc: I designed the logo and visual identity from scratch — a clean, industrial aesthetic that matches the premium direction the client wanted, while staying simple enough to work at any size, from a favicon to a printed roll-up.
smartspares.challengesDesc1: Elementor has real limits when you're chasing a very specific, premium look — I used custom CSS to build things the page builder couldn't do on its own.
smartspares.challengesDesc2: The biggest challenge wasn't technical — it was matching my cousin's very specific taste, shaped by studying the Apple website closely. Getting that level of polish right took several rounds of refinement.
smartspares.lookbackDesc: This project pushed me to build a real design system — consistent spacing, reusable components, and a clear typographic hierarchy — for a B2B platform that had to feel premium, not just functional. It's one of the projects I'm most proud of, because I handled every part of it myself, from the first sketch to the final line of CSS.

lamara.title: Lamara - Web Design Project | Bilal Essatte
lamara.metaDesc: Discover the Lamara project - Creating an elegant and high-performance web experience.
lamara.heroDesc: Development of an elegant web platform for Lamara, combining refined aesthetics and advanced features for an exceptional user experience.
lamara.contextDesc: Lamara wanted to stand out in a competitive market with an online presence that reflects their premium positioning. The goal was to create a web experience combining visual elegance and technical performance, while facilitating discovery of their products and services.
lamara.approachIntro: I developed a complete web solution that showcases Lamara's identity through:
lamara.approachItem1: Elegant user interface with smooth animations
lamara.approachItem2: Responsive architecture adapted to all screens
lamara.impactDesc: The results speak for themselves — here's what this project achieved.

allinfes.title: Allinfes — Hospitality Sourcing Platform | Bilal Essatte
allinfes.metaDesc: Design and development of a WordPress WooCommerce platform for Allinfes, a Moroccan hospitality sourcing brand.
allinfes.heroDesc: Design and development of a B2B e-commerce platform for the Moroccan hospitality sourcing brand — a complete system enabling hotels and riads to source their equipment directly online.
allinfes.challengeDesc: Allinfes, a Moroccan brand specialized in hospitality sourcing, had no digital presence capable of serving international B2B clients. Hotels and riads could not access the catalog, request quotes, or place orders online.
allinfes.solutionDesc: Development of a custom WordPress + WooCommerce platform with product catalog organized by categories, quote request system, and interface designed to inspire confidence in international buyers.
allinfes.processPhase1Desc: Analysis of the Moroccan hospitality sourcing sector. Definition of information architecture, product categories, and user journey for international B2B buyers.
allinfes.processPhase2Desc: Design of a premium interface reflecting Allinfes' high-end positioning. Typography, color palette and visual system designed to inspire confidence in international clients.
allinfes.processPhase3Desc: WordPress + Elementor + WooCommerce development with quote request workflow, product category organization, and performance optimization for fast loading.
allinfes.ctaDesc: I design and develop web platforms that convert — from showcase sites to complete B2B e-commerce.

mielchahda.title: Miel Chahda — E-Commerce Turnaround | Bilal Essatte
mielchahda.metaDesc: Full WordPress e-commerce recovery for Miel Chahda: speed optimization, WooCommerce restructuring, homepage rebuild and SEO — desktop performance from 49 to 71.
mielchahda.heroDesc: A full technical recovery and redesign for a Moroccan honey cooperative — fixing a site left in critical condition by a previous developer, without a single hour of downtime on live orders.
mielchahda.aboutDesc1: Coopérative Agricole CHAHDA is a Moroccan honey producer based in El Jadida. I originally built their e-commerce site in 2020. In 2024, the site went offline due to sales challenges. When the owner relaunched, he hired another developer — who left the site in critical condition: over 30 active plugins generating conflicting scripts, uncompressed images weighing up to 3 MB each, broken category structures, and a mobile performance score of 41/100 with a 72.5 second Largest Contentful Paint.
mielchahda.aboutDesc2: The owner brought me back to fix it. The challenge wasn't rebuilding the site — it was fixing it without taking it down, while live Cash-on-Delivery orders were still coming in.
mielchahda.infoToolsValue: WordPress, WooCommerce, Elementor, Rank Math SEO, LiteSpeed Cache, Gemini AI, Canva
mielchahda.perfDesc: Real numbers from PageSpeed Insights, before and after Phase 1.
mielchahda.phase1Item1: Configured LiteSpeed Cache and enabled browser caching
mielchahda.phase1Item2: Set up WebP image delivery via QUIC.cloud
mielchahda.phase1Item3: Compressed raw images from 1–3 MB down to 100–300 KB
mielchahda.phase1Item4: Minified and deferred non-critical JS/CSS while keeping WooCommerce checkout intact
mielchahda.phase1Item5: Identified a heavy slider plugin as the primary mobile bottleneck and removed it
mielchahda.phase1Item6: Safely deactivated conflicting plugins without disrupting live Cash-on-Delivery orders
mielchahda.phase2Item1: Completely rebuilt the homepage layout in Elementor
mielchahda.phase2Item2: Removed artificial urgency timers, duplicate sections and clutter
mielchahda.phase2Item3: Created 'Nos Variétés de Miel' section with 12 custom botanical illustrations
mielchahda.phase2Item4: Integrated 4 client testimonials and fixed the Coffret Chahda section
mielchahda.phase2Item5: Audited full WooCommerce catalog — deleted 15 invalid/duplicate products
mielchahda.phase2Item6: Created 6 clean product categories: Collection du Miel, Amlou Marocain, Mélange du Miel, Produits de la Ruche, Fruits Secs, Pâte à Tartiner
mielchahda.phase2Item7: Configured exact weight variations (125g / 250g / 500g / 1kg) for all products
mielchahda.phase2Item8: Retouched and prepared all product images with clean studio-style backgrounds
mielchahda.phase2Item9: Deployed Rank Math SEO across all 37 product pages with original French content
mielchahda.phase2Item10: Updated footer with real contact numbers, location and partner logos (Carrefour, Aswak Salam, ONSSA)
mielchahda.photoDesc: All product images were retouched and prepared for web — original photos edited with clean studio-style backgrounds for a consistent, premium look across the catalog.
mielchahda.lessonQuote: Fixing someone else's mess is harder than building from scratch. Every change carried the risk of breaking a live checkout while real customers were placing orders.
mielchahda.lessonDesc: The previous developer had left the site with over 30 active plugins generating conflicting scripts and layout breaks. Diagnosing which plugin caused which problem — without simply deactivating everything at once — required methodical, isolated testing. Every optimization was verified against live WooCommerce functionality before being kept.
mielchahda.lookbackDesc: This project confirmed something I already suspected: performance optimization and technical debt recovery require a different mindset than new builds. You have to understand what's there before you can improve it — and move carefully, because someone's business is running on it while you work.

omegasushi.title: Omega Sushi Tanger — Branding & Social Media | Bilal Essatte
omegasushi.metaDesc: Complete visual identity, 13-page menu design, AI food retouching, and digital launch for Omega Sushi Tanger — delivered in 1 week.
omegasushi.heroTags: Branding, Menu Design, Social Media, AI Photo Retouching
omegasushi.heroDesc: Complete visual identity, 13-page digital menu, AI food photography retouching, and launch on Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp Business and Glovo — all delivered in one week.
omegasushi.challengeDesc: Omega Sushi Tanger was opening its doors with no visual identity or digital presence whatsoever. The existing food photos were of poor quality. Everything had to be created from scratch and the restaurant launched online in minimum time.
omegasushi.solutionDesc: Complete visual ecosystem delivered in 1 week — logo from scratch, 13-page digital menu, food photos retouched via Gemini Nano Banana Pro, and deployment on Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp Business and Glovo.
omegasushi.metric1Desc: Complete delivery time — logo, 13-page menu, social media, photo retouching
omegasushi.metric2Desc: Mediocre quality food photos transformed into professional visuals via Nano Banana Pro
omegasushi.metric3Desc: Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp Business connected and operational from launch
omegasushi.processPhase1Desc: Analysis of Japanese restaurant industry trends via Gemini Deep Research. Definition of a modern visual direction — stylized navy blue samurai in an orange circle — combining Japanese aesthetics with a strong identity adapted to the Tangier market.
omegasushi.processPhase2Desc: Logo creation from scratch in Adobe Illustrator. Development of complete brand guidelines. Design of a 13-page digital menu in Canva/Illustrator. Professional food photo retouching via Gemini Nano Banana Pro — transforming mediocre photos into premium visuals ready for Glovo and Instagram.
omegasushi.processPhase3Desc: Creation and configuration of Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp Business pages, connected via Meta Business Suite. Production of Instagram content (promotional offers, food photos). Optimized copywriting with ChatGPT to maximize engagement and organic reach.
omegasushi.ctaDesc: I create complete visual identities and digital strategies for restaurants and F&B brands — with short deadlines and premium results.
